notes: mistral-vibe mid-turn queue. Vibe's TUI queue (message_queue.py) holds a mid-turn follow-up IN MEMORY: enqueue_prompt writes nothing to disk, and start_drain_if_needed returns early while agent_running(), so the queued message reaches messages.jsonl ONLY when the drain dequeues it and starts a real turn — after turn 1's turn_done. Turn 1's working span is therefore contiguous (no synthetic user-line, no →ready flicker when the follow-up is typed). Unlike claudecode, vibe never folds the queued text into the current turn — the drain is gated on agent-not-running, so it always runs as a distinct subsequent turn: hence turn1_settle (ready) → queued_turn_start (working) → queued_turn_end (ready) are modeled explicitly. BACKFLOW (live basic-turn recording): mistral-vibe is a PRESESSION adapter — a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready BEFORE the real session_<id> transcript dir reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id; modeled as presession_birth → session_birth(new_session) → pid_bind, every later phase same_session_as session_birth (birth templated from the codex sibling). Offline replay cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar, so model + tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — observations assert agent only (cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false), matching kiro-cli. PROVISIONAL on live emission ordering — record settles the two-turn interleaving. KNOWN_FAILING (recording 2026-07-07-22-26-37_irrlichd-0.5.5+8929eb7.dirty, live vibe 2.19.0): queued_window PASSES (turn 1 held working 3570ms via a bash tool, follow-up held in-memory, no flicker), but queued_turn_start/end FAIL. vibe drains message_queue synchronously when agent_running() clears, so the queued turn runs with no ready gap; the daemon debounce-coalesces turn 1 + the queued turn into ONE working->ready span (single ready 22:26:58.529). The queued turn is a genuine distinct turn in messages.jsonl (user 6x7 -> assistant 42) but the daemon does not surface a second working->ready. Daemon coalescing bug, issue prepared; when the daemon splits the coalesced turns, drop this flag.

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<ts>_<hash> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
First prompt (slow count-to-10) drives ready → working; turn 1 begins on the real session

A plain-text follow-up is typed into the TUI mid-turn-1 and held in vibe's in-memory queue (never written to messages.jsonl until dequeued). State must stay working through the rest of turn 1 — no synthetic user-line flicker to ready, no flicker to waiting
Turn 1 completes (terminal text-only assistant → turn_done) → ready. The queued follow-up has NOT yet landed in the transcript

The drain engine (agent no longer running) dequeues the queued message and starts a NEW turn — this is when the queued user line lands in messages.jsonl, driving ready → working at a legitimate turn boundary (not a mid-turn flicker)
The queued turn completes → final state ready (the agent consumes the queued message and the cycle still ends at ready)
